steps.title: Identify the grammatical relationship across the blank
steps.content: The structure is: an independent clause about interactions occurring earlier, followed by a noun phrase “a finding that would overturn theories” elaborating on it. This noun phrase is an appositive.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for appositives
steps.content:
The rule: A comma separates an independent clause from an appositive noun phrase. Choice C correctly uses a comma to mark this relationship. The other options fail: A omits punctuation, B uses a period (fragmenting the sentence), and D uses a semicolon (which requires independent clauses on both sides, but the phrase “a finding that...” is a noun phrase).
That matches choice C — C is correct.
